title: Sampling of Tooth Roots for Ancient DNA
author: Marcel Keller
materials: Wipe down markers and pens with DNA Exitus.
For the next sample, start from step 9.

steps.title: Place down sheets of aluminum foil...
steps.instruction: Place down sheets of aluminum foil to cover the entire surface of the drill hood.

steps.title: Make an L-shaped “wall” of folded...
steps.instruction: Make an L-shaped “wall” of folded foil that runs along the back of your work area and the side without the drill to keep flying dust from expanding across the hood.

steps.title: Place down a smaller piece of...
steps.instruction: Place down a smaller piece of foil for your working surface. Fold small pieces of foil that you can wrap around the beaks of the tooth extractor. Place a big weighing boat onto the working surface. Change this setup for each sample.

steps.title: Gather clean, decontaminated tools for drilling...
steps.instruction: Gather clean, decontaminated tools for drilling and place them on the foil.

steps.title: Make sure your drill bit/wheel is...
steps.instruction: Make sure your drill bit/wheel is centered and firmly in the drill before drilling and that your foot is off the pedal when not intending to drill.

steps.title: Turn on the drill in “FWD”...
steps.instruction: Turn on the drill in “FWD” mode and choose a medium speed to prevent overheating/burning of the bone or dentine.

steps.title: Put on clean sleeves by pushing...
steps.instruction: Put on clean sleeves by pushing your thumbs through the plastic close to the cuffs to keep the sleeve in place, then gloves on top.

steps.title: Turn the hood on full power and open the glass
steps.instruction: Turn the hood on full power and open the glass.

steps.title: Label your bags and tubes
steps.instruction: Label your bags and tubes: Tubes should be closed immediately after taking them from the package and opened only during sampling. This will limit contamination. A B 1 Bags for drilled samples 2 Sample ID ABC001A 3 Date DD/MM/YYYY 4 Initials YZ A B C 1 Tubes e.g. for collecting calculus Top Side 2 Content e.g. calculus/drill powder 3 Project ID PROJ PROJ 4 Sample ID ABC001A ABC001A 5 Date DD/MM/YYYY DD/MM/YYYY 6 Initials YZ YZ

steps.title: To decontaminate drill bits, drill spanner,...
steps.instruction: To decontaminate drill bits, drill spanner, tweezers, dental scalers and toothbrush – Soak in 6% bleach for 5 minutes, rinse with MilliQ water, rinse with 70% Ethanol. Clean the drill spanner and tooth extractors with DNA Exitus.

steps.title: Set up decontamination station
steps.instruction: Set up decontamination station: 4 disposable 100 ml beakers 6% NaOCl (bleach) aliquot [2x] MilliQ water [1x] 70% ethanol aliquot [1x]

steps.title: Clean drill hood and table bench...
steps.instruction: Clean drill hood and table bench surfaces with DNA Exitus and wipe clean with a paper towel.

steps.title: Aim to do all work as...
steps.instruction: Aim to do all work as far into the hood as possible to avoid dust escaping and keep eyes behind the glass for protection.

steps.title: Place the bag with your sample...
steps.instruction: Place the bag with your sample and the clean sample bag and tube into the hood behind the foil wall.

steps.title: If the tooth has calculus, use...
steps.instruction: If the tooth has calculus, use the dental scaler to remove it before sampling. Put the tooth into the mini grip bag and hold the tooth outside the bag with your fingers. Now push gently with scaler and collect all calculus inside the bag. Remove the tooth and pour the calculus on weighing paper. Use weighing paper to place calculus in a labeled 1.5 ml tube after taring.

steps.title: Wrap foil strips around the ends...
steps.instruction: Wrap foil strips around the ends of your tooth extractor (for holding the tooth).

steps.title: Hold the tooth with the tooth...
steps.instruction: Hold the tooth with the tooth extractor and drill carefully a few millimeters above the end of the root.

steps.title: When drilling is completed, put your samples in labeled bags
steps.instruction: When drilling is completed, put your samples in labeled bags. Wipe these down with a small amount of DNA Exitus before adding to your sample box.

steps.title: Rinse off the drill wheel with...
steps.instruction: Rinse off the drill wheel with MilliQ water first then into your 6% bleach for a few minutes or while you are drilling the next tooth. Then rinse with water and finally ethanol before drying for use again.

steps.title: Carefully remove and throw away the...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and throw away the weighing boat, small work surface foil, dirty gloves, and sleeves. Put on new gloves.

steps.title: When finished for the day, wipe...
steps.instruction: When finished for the day, wipe down the tooth extractors and drill spanner with DNA Exitus and put away.

steps.title: Use a clean toothbrush with a...
steps.instruction: Use a clean toothbrush with a little DNA Exitus to remove sample powder from the top/inside of the drill head. Do not use bleach! This will rust the inner components.

steps.title: Leave drill bits and scalers to...
steps.instruction: Leave drill bits and scalers to soak in bleach while you throw away all foil and other consumables and wipe down all inner hood work surfaces and the outside of the drill with DNA Exitus and wipe dry with paper towels.
